session.title: Moodle AI Agent Blocker Plugin - Full Project Development
session.topic_summary: An end-to-end open-source Moodle plugin was designed, architected, and iteratively developed to detect and block AI agents (ChatGPT browser mode, Manus AI, Perplexity Comet, Selenium/Puppeteer) from completing academic assessments on behalf of students. The project spans plugin architecture, multi-layer detection algorithms (user-agent fingerprinting, HTTP header analysis, canvas fingerprinting, behavioral timing analysis), database schema design and migrations, GDPR compliance, admin reporting dashboards, and a phased implementation roadmap. Multiple debugging cycles addressed real production issues including broken column headers, download errors, and database upgrade scripting.

axes.label: Backend Dev
axes.description: PHP/Moodle plugin development depth including hooks, observers, and AMD JS

axes.label: Security Design
axes.description: Multi-layer AI agent detection design, scoring systems, and evasion awareness

axes.label: Data Engineering
axes.description: Schema design, upgrade scripting, reporting queries, and data normalization

axes.label: Compliance
axes.description: GDPR privacy provider, data retention policy, and student rights considerations

axes.label: Strategic Thinking
axes.description: Phased roadmap, trade-off analysis (blocking vs flagging), success KPI definition

axes.label: Documentation
axes.description: Volume and quality of technical docs, changelogs, debug guides, and schema docs

blooms_progression.label: Remember
blooms_progression.title: Moodle Standards Recall
blooms_progression.description: Correctly recalled Moodle plugin naming conventions (local_aiagentblock), required files (version.php, db/install.xml), GPL v3 licensing requirements, and Moodle 4.0–5.1 compatibility matrix.

blooms_progression.label: Understand
blooms_progression.title: AI Agent Threat Modeling
blooms_progression.description: Demonstrated understanding of how AI agents (ChatGPT browser mode, Manus, Comet) operate, what signals they leak, and why traditional plagiarism detection misses them. Accurately explained canvas fingerprinting and CV% timing analysis concepts.

blooms_progression.label: Apply
blooms_progression.title: Plugin Implementation
blooms_progression.description: Applied Moodle event system, observer pattern, AMD JavaScript modules, and Mustache templating to build a functional multi-layer detection plugin. Applied database upgrade scripting patterns to add new columns without data loss.

blooms_progression.label: Analyze
blooms_progression.title: Bug Diagnosis & Root Cause
blooms_progression.description: Analyzed production issues including broken column headers (placeholder strings), download errors from missing output buffering, and misaligned DB schema. Correctly identified cache invalidation as the most likely root cause in 90% of cases.

blooms_progression.label: Evaluate
blooms_progression.title: Design Trade-off Judgment
blooms_progression.description: Evaluated competing design priorities: blocking vs. flagging, privacy vs. surveillance depth, strict vs. lenient detection sensitivity. Formulated success metrics (>80% detection rate, <2% false positive rate) and identified genuine limitations of the system.

blooms_progression.label: Create
blooms_progression.title: Original Plugin Authorship
blooms_progression.description: Created an original, publishable-quality Moodle plugin from scratch including full directory structure, database schema, PHP classes (detector, logger, observer), JavaScript detection module, Mustache templates, admin settings, language strings, PHPUnit test scaffolding, and comprehensive documentation suite.

timeline.title: Project Conception & Requirements
timeline.description: Defined the problem (AI agents completing student assessments), identified target AI agents (ChatGPT, Manus, Comet), and established plugin scope and Moodle version targets.

timeline.title: Architecture & Repository Design
timeline.description: Designed the full plugin directory structure, selected GPL v3 licensing, mapped Moodle plugin conventions, and established GitHub branching strategy and tagging.

timeline.title: Core Detection Engine Development
timeline.description: Built the multi-layer detection system: user-agent string matching, HTTP header analysis, client-side canvas fingerprinting, and JavaScript behavioral monitoring with weighted suspicion scoring.

timeline.title: Database Schema Design
timeline.description: Designed install.xml schema for detection log table, course-level settings table, and activity-level settings table. Implemented GDPR-compliant data structure with retention considerations.

timeline.title: Quiz-Specific Detection & Timing Analysis
timeline.description: Added server-side timing analysis using CV% (coefficient of variation) of inter-action timing to distinguish robotic from human behavior. Added quiz-specific Moodle event hooks.

timeline.title: Reporting Dashboard
timeline.description: Built sortable admin report with color-coded badges for CV%, grade, suspicion score, and steps-per-page metrics. Implemented CSV/Excel download functionality.

timeline.title: Bug Fixes & Schema Migration
timeline.description: Debugged broken column headers (placeholder string keys), fixed output buffering download errors, wrote db/upgrade.php to migrate existing installs to new schema without data loss.

timeline.title: Privacy, Testing & Documentation
timeline.description: Implemented GDPR privacy provider, scaffolded PHPUnit tests, authored comprehensive docs suite (installation, configuration, detection methods, schema upgrade guide, future development).

employer_value.title: Security-First Software Delivery
employer_value.description: Designed and built a production-ready security plugin from scratch, demonstrating ability to identify real-world threat vectors, architect multi-signal detection systems, and balance security effectiveness with false-positive risk.

employer_value.title: Full-Stack Open Source Contribution
employer_value.description: Produced a complete, standards-compliant Moodle plugin including backend PHP, AMD JavaScript, database schemas, Mustache templates, language strings, and PHPUnit tests — demonstrating breadth across the full LMS development stack.

employer_value.title: Data-Driven Feature Design
employer_value.description: Designed an analytics reporting layer with sortable metrics, color-coded thresholds, and CSV export — turning raw detection logs into actionable intelligence for educators reviewing academic integrity cases.

employer_value.title: Compliance & Ethical Engineering
employer_value.description: Proactively incorporated GDPR compliance (privacy provider, data retention, student disclosure requirements) and articulated genuine ethical trade-offs around surveillance vs. privacy in an educational setting.

employer_value.title: Strategic Product Thinking
employer_value.description: Authored a phased 8-week implementation roadmap with defined success KPIs, rollback plans, and open design questions — demonstrating product management capability beyond pure engineering execution.

meta.notes: Analysis based on project knowledge base containing 10+ documentation files spanning the full plugin lifecycle. Duration estimated at 8 hours across multiple sessions given the volume of distinct artifacts produced (plugin code, 7+ docs, schema files, debug guides). Scores reflect demonstrated competency in session artifacts, not claimed mastery. Knowledge value USD estimate based on equivalent Moodle developer consulting rates (~$80–100/hr for 52 LMS-equivalent hours). False-positive risk on meta-cognition score (65) — the project knowledge does not surface explicit self-reflection moments, only applied decision-making.
